message:
chore(beads): close bd-5h4xq bd-6vfgs bd-ttwfr + bd-rzs2k surfaced

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -459,7 +459,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-5gnk.1","title":"Replace hard-coded glob_t struct offsets with proper type access","description":"FILE: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/string_abi.rs:4706-4794
CURRENT: Uses raw byte offsets for glob_t fields:
  - offset 0: gl_pathc (usize)
  - offset 8: gl_pathv (*mut *mut c_char)
  - offset 16: gl_offs (usize)
These are correct for x86_64 but fragile.

FIX: Define a #[repr(C)] GlobT struct matching libc::glob_t layout, or use libc::glob_t directly if available. Access fields through the struct instead of raw pointer arithmetic.
IMPACT: Low — x86_64 only for now, but will matter for ARM64 port.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:41.877066358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:44:30.145394818Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:44:30.144423169Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5gnk.1","depends_on_id":"bd-5gnk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:41.877066358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-5gnk.2","title":"Fix case-insensitive IP comparison in reverse_lookup_hosts","description":"FILE: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/resolv/mod.rs:111
CURRENT: eq_ignore_ascii_case(&line_addr, addr) for IP address comparison
PROBLEM: IP addresses are numeric — case-insensitive comparison is semantically wrong even though it produces correct results (digits have no case).
FIX: Use direct byte comparison: line_addr == addr
IMPACT: Zero — functionally identical. Pure code correctness.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:43.096638342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:28:00.424613033Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:28:00.423655881Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5gnk.2","depends_on_id":"bd-5gnk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:43.096638342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-5gnk.3","title":"Standardize errno handling pattern across all ABI modules","description":"The audit found inconsistent errno handling across modules:
- Some use std::io::Error::last_os_error().raw_os_error().unwrap_or(default)
- Some use crate::host_resolve::host_errno(default)
- Some read libc::__errno_location() directly
- Some forget to set errno on error paths

STANDARD PATTERN (already used in most places):
  let e = std::io::Error::last_os_error().raw_os_error().unwrap_or(default_errno);
  unsafe { set_abi_errno(e) };

AUDIT: Grep for all error return paths (-1, NULL, EOF) and verify each sets errno.
This was partially done during the audit but a systematic pass would catch remaining gaps.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:44.276235737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:48:11.055032005Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:48:11.054069683Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5gnk.3","depends_on_id":"bd-5gnk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:44.276235737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-5jsr","title":"resolv: gai_suspend degenerate request sets should ignore timeout validation and preserve errno","description":"Host glibc returns EAI_ALLDONE with errno unchanged for gai_suspend on already-complete degenerate request sets even when the timeout is invalid (for example tv_nsec=1_000_000_000 or tv_sec<0). FrankenLibC currently matches that behavior, but the ABI regression suite only covers NULL timeout cases. Add focused tests and document the timeout-ignored semantics in unistd_abi.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:50:23.785286766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:53:14.283181440Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:53:14.280876516Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity gai_suspend timeout semantics for already-complete degenerate request sets, verifying that invalid timespec values are ignored and errno stays unchanged; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-5ky","title":"Harness: Regression report for strict vs hardened (latency/risk Pareto)","description":"Create a report that summarizes tradeoffs.

Contents:
- Strict vs hardened deltas in latency (p50/p95) and risk/repair rates.
- Pareto cumulative regret trends.
- Any FDR/alpha-investing and approachability signals.

Goal:
- Make it obvious when a change improves safety but violates latency budget, or vice versa.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:42.328425057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T18:02:08.192491089Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T18:02:08.192472995Z","close_reason":"Added strict-vs-hardened runtime_math regression report (per-mode subprocess metrics + Markdown/JSON output)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5ky","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5ky","depends_on_id":"bd-3aa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-5rs1","title":"syscall: fsmount invalid fsfd should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"On the current host, unprivileged fsmount(-1, 0, 0) and fsmount(-1, 1, 0) fail with EPERM rather than EBADF/EINVAL. Add focused ABI parity coverage so this privilege-first behavior stays explicit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:49:55.553436878Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:56:26.613206683Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:56:26.611053276Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add an exact ABI regression assertion so message-send null-payload handling stops hiding behind a broad errno family.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:26:15.934020928Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:16:36.884354820Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:16:36.882046430Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-6n9x","title":"syscall: fsopen nonzero flags with valid fsname should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"Host glibc returns EPERM for fsopen("tmpfs", 1) on this unprivileged host.  {"id":"bd-6xwq","title":"membrane: execute membrane_mode_split.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/membrane_mode_split_conformance_test.rs validates tests/conformance/fixtures/membrane_mode_split.json structurally but never executes the strict/hardened divergence cases via the harness subprocess. Add conformance-matrix-case execution for the membrane mode-split fixtures, assert the expected UB vs repaired outputs, and fix any runtime-mode parity regressions surfaced by the new executable coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T05:56:34.371460296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:05:15.109883796Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:05:15.107705573Z","close_reason":"Executed membrane_mode_split fixture coverage through the isolated harness conformance matrix path and verified with rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-6yd","title":"Crash bundle: deterministic evidence + backtrace capture on failure","description":"Critique mapping: #1 (prove; do not hand-wave).

Deliverables:
- When any smoke/fixture fails, capture:
  - backtrace (RUST_BACKTRACE=1)
  - mode + env vars
  - evidence ledger snapshot (TSM repairs/denials)
  - allocator stats snapshot (arena/quarantine)
  - /proc/self/maps excerpt for pointer provenance debugging

Acceptance:
- Bundle generation is deterministic and bounded (no unbounded logs).
- Bundle is sufficient to reproduce the failure locally.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): crash_bundle_test (9 pass) + gate script. Artifact filenames, bounds, determinism rules, and reproduction checklist all validated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:26.930667114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","close_reason":"Crash bundle specification operational. 9 harness tests pass. check_crash_bundle.sh validates evidence snapshots, determinism rules, and reproduction checklist.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","critique","diagnostics"]}
 {"id":"bd-6z3m","title":"syscall: move_mount NULL target pathname should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"On the current host, unprivileged move_mount(AT_FDCWD, "/", AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0) fails with EPERM. Add focused ABI parity coverage so this privilege-first path remains explicit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:44:07.162600221Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:12:14.739269016Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:12:14.736925699Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -823,7 +823,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-rth1","title":"bd-yos subtask: TLS handoff and teardown correctness for spawned threads","description":"Background:
- TLS handoff correctness is required for thread-local errno and future pthread_key destructor behavior.

Goal:
- Implement and verify TLS initialization/teardown handoff for spawned threads.

Deliverables:
1) TLS init protocol during thread start.
2) Per-thread errno/TLS storage linkage checks.
3) Teardown sequencing and cleanup invariants.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Thread-local values remain isolated and stable under concurrent workloads.
- TLS teardown leaves no leaked state.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for TLS setup/teardown and isolation.
- E2E concurrent tests validating cross-thread isolation.
- Structured logs with trace_id, thread_id, tls_event, key_count, cleanup_status.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.128037997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:33.353261867Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:33.353242521Z","close_reason":"Completed TLS handoff/teardown correctness fixes and verification evidence posted in comments.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","tls"],"comments":[{"id":1142,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Codex claiming this now. Found a concrete TLS table correctness bug: removing one TID currently breaks linear-probe lookup chains for colliding TIDs, which can corrupt per-thread TLS lookup after teardown. I am implementing tombstone-based delete + collision tests now.","created_at":"2026-02-22T05:19:32Z"},{"id":1143,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Codex","text":"Completed TLS handoff/teardown hardening and verification.

Implemented:
- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in  (tombstone semantics preserve lookup chains).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback () to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.
- Added ABI integration tests in :
  - 
  - 
  - shared guard + native-threading force helper for deterministic setup.

[31m-    let total_size = GUARD_PAGE_SIZE.checked_add(stack_size).ok_or(crate::errno::ENOMEM)?;
(B[m[32m+    let total_size = GUARD_PAGE_SIZE
(B[m[32m+        .checked_add(stack_size)
(B[m[32m+        .ok_or(crate::errno::ENOMEM)?;
(B[m 
     // Allocate the full region as read+write.
     // SAFETY: anonymous mmap with no fd, valid parameters. ✅

- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/tls.rs` (tombstone semantics preserve collision-chain lookups).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback (`thread_local!`) to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.
- Added ABI TSD integration coverage in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_tsd_test.rs`:
  - `tsd_isolated_across_concurrent_threads`

  - serialized native-threading setup helper.

 {"id":"bd-s170","title":"Smoke-fuzz proptests for resolver parsers","description":"Archetype 1 (Crash Detector) via proptest for 4 resolver parsers that consume arbitrary bytes: parse_hosts_line, parse_services_line, ResolverConfig::parse, encode_domain_name. Each parser must never panic, must terminate on any byte sequence, and must produce outputs bounded relative to the input size (no infinite expansion). One parser per commit. Oracle: crash-free + size-bounded output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:16:44.821539800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:23:12.951422556Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:23:12.949455593Z","close_reason":"Added 13 smoke-fuzz proptests across 4 commits covering all 4 resolver parsers: 899230c (parse_hosts_line 3 tests), db6c4a4 (parse_services_line 3 tests), 96fae93 (ResolverConfig::parse 3 tests), 547c70e (encode_domain_name 4 tests). Each target has: (a) never-panics on arbitrary bytes, (b) structural invariant check, (c) where applicable, biased structured-alphabet fuzz to reach deep parser paths. ~6500 case evaluations per run.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-s39o","title":"EPIC: Expand LD_PRELOAD program compatibility beyond 61/61 core suite","description":"FrankenLibC currently passes 61/61 programs in the core LD_PRELOAD test suite PLUS 12+ complex programs (Python, Perl, Node.js, SSH, OpenSSL, Bash, Zsh, Vim, less, nano, curl, make, tar, gzip).

GOAL: Systematically expand compatibility to cover more real-world workloads. The next targets should be programs that exercise features we haven't fully tested:
- Database clients (psql, mysql, sqlite3)
- Build tools (cmake, ninja, gcc, rustc)
- Network tools (wget, dig, nslookup)
- Compression (bzip2, xz, zstd)
- Package managers (apt, dpkg)
- Editors/pagers (emacs, man)

APPROACH: Test each program, capture failure backtraces, fix root causes. Each fix typically benefits many programs.

ALREADY WORKING (verified):
61 core: cat ls echo wc head tail sort grep sed awk date env id uname whoami hostname pwd basename dirname mkdir rmdir touch rm cp mv ln chmod stat file dd tr cut paste fold uniq comm tee xargs yes seq expr test true false printf readlink realpath nproc getconf stty tty logname uptime arch git df du free kill sleep nice
Complex: python3 perl node curl make tar gzip ssh openssl bash zsh vim less nano","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PlumCove","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:51.740882739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T19:47:50.422019583Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T19:47:50.421726608Z","close_reason":"All children closed: bd-s39o.1 (database clients), bd-s39o.2 (build tools), bd-s39o.3 (network tools). 61/61 core suite + 12+ complex programs pass.  {"id":"bd-thm2","title":"Fix c11threads managed-attr/host-pthread init mismatch","description":"c11threads_abi::mtx_init was building a FrankenLibC-managed pthread mutexattr word and passing it into host pthread_mutex_init, which triggered a glibc __pthread_tpp_change_priority assertion in c11threads_abi_test. Fix the C11 mutex/condvar init lane so C11 synchronization objects are initialized consistently on the native-managed path, then verify the focused C11 suite and workspace ABI checks via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilentFrog","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:34:41.394889788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54.204943932Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54.204703667Z","close_reason":"Resolved C11 mutex/condvar init mismatch by keeping C11 synchronization initialization on the native-managed lane; focused c11threads tests now pass and workspace tests advance to unrelated dlfcn failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1358,"issue_id":"bd-thm2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented scoped native-mutex delegation helpers in pthread_abi and switched c11threads mutex/condvar init to use the native-managed lane. Verification: `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test c11threads_abi_test test_mtx_init_lock_unlock_destroy -- --exact --nocapture --test-threads=1` passed; `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test c11threads_abi_test -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` passed (36/36); `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` passed; `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passed; `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` completed without diffs. Workspace-wide `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` is still running under rch session 98302.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:36:15Z"},{"id":1359,"issue_id":"bd-thm2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Broader verification update: `rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets` no longer aborts in c11threads; the next remaining workspace failure is unrelated in `-p frankenlibc-abi --test dlfcn_abi_test` (14 failures, first at `dlerror_consumed_after_read`, then lock poisoning across dlopen/dlsym/dlvsym tests). This confirms the c11threads glibc assertion is resolved.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-tvug","title":"Complementary mount-family veneer error tests (open_tree/fsmount/mount_setattr)","description":"Cod's bd-rp9r (commit e6e612a) added syscall number + signature pins for 7 mount-namespace syscalls plus 2 error tests (setns/unshare).
